name="keifu6"/>）、[[内色許売命]]<ref name="kamei"/><ref name="oshikoji"/><ref name="shinbetsu"/><ref name="keifu1"/><ref name="keifu2"/><ref name="keifu4"/><ref name="keifu5"/><ref name="keifu3"/><ref name="tenson"/><ref name="keifu6"/>、大峯大尼命<ref name="oshikoji"/><ref name="shinbetsu"/><ref name="keifu2"/><ref name="keifu3"/>（大峰大尼命<ref name="tenson"/>） | 妻 = 新河小楯媛命<ref name="kamei"/><ref name="shinbetsu"/><ref name="keifu1"/><ref name="keifu4"/><ref name="keifu5"/>、芹田真稚姫<ref name="oshikoji"/><ref name="keifu3"/><ref name="tenson"/> | 子 = [[大水口宿禰命]]<ref name="kamei"/><ref name="oshikoji"/><ref name="shinbetsu"/><ref name="keifu1"/><ref name="keifu2"/><ref name="keifu4"/><ref name="keifu5"/>、大水口比売命<ref name="oshikoji"/><ref name="shinbetsu"/><ref name="keifu2"/>、大矢口命<ref name="keifu2"/>、武建大尼命（芹田真稚姫との子）<ref name="keifu3"/><ref name="tenson"/><ref name="keifu6"/>、一説：[[伊迦賀色許売命]] | 特記事項 = }} '''内色許男命'''（うつしこおのみこと）は、古代[[日本]]の[[豪族]]・'''[[穂積氏|穂積臣]]'''の祖。『[[日本書紀]]』では'''鬱色雄命'''とも表記する。
== 概要 == 『[[古事記]]』の[[孝元天皇]]記において、孝元天皇の后・[[内色許売命]]の兄として登場し、続けて天皇が娘の[[伊迦賀色許売命]]も妃として娶ったとの記述があるが、伊迦賀色許売命を内色許男命の娘とする伝承は『古事記』以外に見られない。『[[日本書紀]]』では[[開化天皇]]即位前紀に[[鬱色謎命]]を穂積臣の祖・鬱色雄命の妹と記す。
『記紀』ではそれ以外に目立った活動は伝えられないが、『[[先代旧事本紀]]』の「天皇本紀」では、孝元天皇即位8年春正月に[[物部氏|物部連公]]の祖・[[宇摩志麻治命]]の子孫である欝色雄命を[[大臣]]とし、兄弟の[[大綜杵命]]を大禰としたことが記される。
その他、「物部」系図<ref name="keifu3"/>には孝元朝に大神を奉斎すると見え、『神別系譜』<ref name="shinbetsu"/>では開化朝の人物とされる。
== 系譜 == 母の坂戸由良都媛命は[[三上氏|三上祝]]の祖・[[坂戸毘古命]]の妹で、妻の新河小楯媛命は坂戸毘古命の子・[[国忍富命]]の妹である。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}

== 系譜 == 母の坂戸由良都媛命は[[三上氏|三上祝]]の祖・[[坂戸毘古命]]の妹で、妻の新河小楯媛命は坂戸毘古命の子・[[国忍富命]]の妹である。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[穂積氏]] * [[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:うつしこおのみこと}} [[Category:日本神話の人物]] [[Category:古代日本の人物]] [[Category:穂積氏]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 内色許男命 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[弥生時代]] | 生誕 = 不詳 | 死没 = 不詳 | 改名 = | 別名 = 欝色雄命 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = [[大臣]] | 主君 = [[孝元天皇]]、[[開化天皇]] | 氏族 = [[穂積氏|穂積臣]]、[[采女氏|采女臣]]祖 | 父母 = 父：[[出石心大臣命]]<ref name="kamei">『亀井家譜』東京大学史料編纂所データベース。</ref><ref name="oshikoji">『押小路家譜』東京大学史料編纂所データベース。</ref><ref name="shinbetsu">[[中田憲信]]『神別系譜』、[[東京国立博物館]]デジタルライブラリー。</ref><ref name="keifu1">中田憲信「東国諸国造 天津彦根之裔」『諸系譜』第一冊。</ref><ref name="keifu2">[[鈴木真年]]「物部大連十市部首」『諸系譜』第一冊。</ref><ref name="keifu4">中田憲信「輿石」『諸系譜』第六冊。</ref><ref name="keifu5">中田憲信「三上祝家系」『諸系譜』第二十八冊。</ref>または[[大矢口宿禰命]]<ref name="keifu3">中田憲信「物部」『諸系譜』第四冊。</ref><ref name="tenson">「天孫本紀」『[[先代旧事本紀]]』。</ref>または[[大水口命]]<ref name="keifu6">鈴木真年「物部大連穂積宿祢」『諸系譜』第三十三冊。</ref>、母：坂戸由良都姫<ref name="kamei"/><ref name="shinbetsu"/><ref name="tenson"/><ref name="keifu3"/>（坂戸由良都媛命<ref name="keifu1"/><ref name="keifu4"/><ref name="keifu5"/>） | 兄弟 = [[大綜麻杵命]]<ref name="kamei"/><ref name="oshikoji"/><ref name="shinbetsu"/><ref name="keifu2"/>（大綜杵命<ref name="keifu3"/><ref name="tenson"/>、大閉蘇杵命<ref name="keifu6"/>）、[[内色許売命]]<ref name="kamei"/><ref name="oshikoji"/><ref name="shinbetsu"/><ref name="keifu1"/><ref name="keifu2"/><ref name="keifu4"/><ref name="keifu5"/><ref name="keifu3"/><ref name="tenson"/><ref name="keifu6"/>、大峯大尼命<ref name="oshikoji"/><ref name="shinbetsu"/><ref name="keifu2"/><ref name="keifu3"/>（大峰大尼命<ref name="tenson"/>） | 妻 = 新河小楯媛命<ref name="kamei"/><ref name="shinbetsu"/><ref name="keifu1"/><ref name="keifu4"/><ref name="keifu5"/>、芹田真稚姫<ref name="oshikoji"/><ref name="keifu3"/><ref name="tenson"/> | 子 = [[大水口宿禰命]]<ref name="kamei"/><ref name="oshikoji"/><ref name="shinbetsu"/><ref name="keifu1"/><ref name="keifu2"/><ref name="keifu4"/><ref name="keifu5"/>、大水口比売命<ref name="oshikoji"/><ref name="shinbetsu"/><ref name="keifu2"/>、大矢口命<ref name="keifu2"/>、武建大尼命（芹田真稚姫との子）<ref name="keifu3"/><ref name="tenson"/><ref name="keifu6"/>、一説：[[伊迦賀色許売命]] | 特記事項 = }} '''内色許男命'''（うつしこおのみこと）は、古代[[日本]]の[[豪族]]・'''[[穂積氏|穂積臣]]'''の祖。『[[日本書紀]]』では'''欝色雄命'''とも表記する。
== 概要 == 『[[古事記]]』の[[孝元天皇]]記において、孝元天皇の后の[[内色許売命]]の兄として登場し、続けて天皇が娘の[[伊迦賀色許売命]]も妃として娶ったとの記述があるが、伊迦賀色許売命を内色許男命の娘とする伝承は『古事記』以外に見られない。『[[日本書紀]]』では[[開化天皇]]即位前紀に[[欝色謎命]]を穂積臣の祖・欝色雄命の妹と記す。
『記紀』ではそれ以外に目立った活動は伝えられないが、『[[先代旧事本紀]]』の「天皇本紀」では、孝元天皇即位8年春正月に[[物部氏|物部連公]]の祖・[[宇摩志麻治命]]の子孫である欝色雄命を[[大臣]]とし、兄弟の[[大綜杵命]]を大禰としたことが記される。
その他、「物部」系図<ref name="keifu3"/>には孝元朝に大神を奉斎すると見え、『神別系譜』<ref name="shinbetsu"/>では開化朝の人物とされる。
== 系譜 == 母の坂戸由良都媛命は[[三上氏|三上祝]]の祖・[[坂戸毘古命]]の妹で、妻の新河小楯媛命は坂戸毘古命の子の[[国忍富命]]の妹である。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[穂積氏]] * [[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:うつしこおのみこと}} [[Category:日本神話の人物]] [[Category:古代日本の人物]] [[Category:穂積氏]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

revision.text:
{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 内色許男命 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[弥生時代]]、[[上古]] | 生誕 = 不詳 | 死没 = 不詳 | 改名 = | 別名 = 欝色雄命 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = [[大臣]] | 主君 = [[孝元天皇]]、[[開化天皇]] | 氏族 = [[穂積氏|穂積臣]]、[[采女氏|采女臣]]祖
| 父母 = 父：[[大矢口宿禰命]]<ref name="tenson">「天孫本紀」『[[先代旧事本紀]]』。</ref>、母：坂戸由良都姫<ref name="tenson"/> | 兄弟 = 内色許男命<ref name="tenson"/>、[[内色許売命]]<ref name="tenson"/>、[[大峯大尼命]]<ref name="tenson"/> | 妻 =芹田真稚姫<ref name="tenson"/> | 子 = 武建大尼命（芹田真稚姫との子）<ref name="tenson"/>、一説：[[伊迦賀色許売命]] | 特記事項 = }} '''内色許男命'''（うつしこおのみこと）は、古代[[日本]]の[[豪族]]・'''[[穂積氏|穂積臣]]'''の祖。『[[古事記]]』の表記であり、『[[日本書紀]]』では'''欝色雄命'''と表記する。
== 概要 == 『[[古事記]]』の[[孝元天皇]]記において、孝元天皇の后の[[欝色謎命|内色許売命]]の兄として登場し、続けて天皇が娘の[[伊香色謎命|伊迦賀色許売命]]も妃として娶ったとの記述があるが、伊迦賀色許売命を内色許男命の娘とする伝承は『古事記』以外に見られない。『[[日本書紀]]』では[[開化天皇]]即位前紀に[[欝色謎命]]を穂積臣の祖・欝色雄命の妹と記す。
『記紀』ではそれ以外に目立った活動は伝えられないが、『[[先代旧事本紀]]』の「天皇本紀」では、孝元天皇即位8年春正月に[[物部氏|物部連公]]の祖・[[ウマシマジ|宇摩志麻治命]]の子孫である欝色雄命を[[大臣]]とし、兄弟の[[大綜麻杵命|大綜杵命]]を大禰としたことが記される。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist|refs= <ref name="keifu3">中田憲信「物部」『諸系譜』第四冊。</ref> <ref name="shinbetsu">[[中田憲信]]『神別系譜』、[[東京国立博物館]]デジタルライブラリー。</ref> }}
== 関連項目 == * [[穂積氏]] * [[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:うつしこおのみこと}} [[Category:日本神話の人物]] [[Category:古代日本の人物]] [[Category:穂積氏]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

『記紀』ではそれ以外に目立った活動は伝えられないが、『[[先代旧事本紀]]』の「天皇本紀」では、孝元天皇即位8年春正月に[[物部氏|物部連公]]の祖・[[ウマシマジ|宇摩志麻治命]]の子孫である欝色雄命を[[大臣]]とし、兄弟の[[大綜麻杵命|大綜杵命]]を大禰としたことが記される。

revision.text:
{{Infobox Noble | name = Utsushikoo no Mikoto | image = | caption = | period = [[Yayoi period]], [[Ancient Japan]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | alias = Utsushikoo no Mikoto (欝色雄命) | posthumous_name = | deified_name = | burial_place = | rank = [[Ōomi]] (Grand Minister) | sovereign = [[Emperor Kōgen]], [[Emperor Kaika]] | clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}, {{ill|Uneme clan|ja|采女氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja|大矢口宿禰命}}"Tenson Hongi" in {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}.
Mother: Sakadoyuratsuhime | siblings = {{ill|Utsushikome no Mikoto|ja|欝色謎命}}, Ōmine Ōni no Mikoto | spouse = Serida Maji Hime | children = Take Tate Ōni no Mikoto (child with Serida Maji Hime), possibly {{ill|Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto|ja|伊迦賀色許売命}} | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} }} '''Utsushikoo no Mikoto''' (内色許男命) is a figure in ancient [[Japan]], identified as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} and referred to as "Utsushikoo no Mikoto" in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and as "Utsushikoo no Mikoto" (欝色雄命) in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}.'' == Overview == In the ''Kojiki'' entry for {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇}}, Utsushikoo no Mikoto is described as the elder brother of the emperor’s consort, {{ill|Utsushikome no Mikoto|ja|欝色謎命}}, and as the father of {{ill|Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto|ja|伊迦賀色許売命}}, whom Emperor Kōgen later married. However, traditions associating Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto as the daughter of Utsushikoo no Mikoto are not found in sources other than the ''Kojiki.'' The ''Nihon Shoki'' refers to Utsushikoo as the brother of Utsushikome no Mikoto and founder of the Hozumi clan. Although the ''Kojiki'' and ''Nihon Shoki'' provide limited accounts of his activities, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' states that in the 8th year of Emperor Kōgen’s reign, Utsushikoo no Mikoto was appointed as Grand Minister (Ōomi), while his brother {{ill|Ōsonomakine no Mikoto|ja|大綜杵命}} was made Chief Minister (Ōnori). == Notes == {{reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} [[Category:Japanese mythology figures]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese figures]] [[Category:Hozumi clan]] [[Category:Date of birth unknown]] [[Category:Date of death unknown]] [[ja:内色許男命]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox Noble | name = Utsushikoo no Mikoto | image = | caption = | period = [[Yayoi period]], [[Ancient Japan]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | alias = Utsushikoo no Mikoto (欝色雄命) | posthumous_name = | deified_name = | burial_place = | rank = [[Ōomi]] (Grand Minister) | sovereign = [[Emperor Kōgen]], [[Emperor Kaika]] | clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}, {{ill|Uneme clan|ja|采女氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja|大矢口宿禰命}}"Tenson Hongi" in {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}.
Mother: Sakadoyuratsuhime | siblings = {{ill|Utsushikome|lt=Utsushikome no Mikoto|ja|欝色謎命}}, Ōmine Ōni no Mikoto | spouse = Serida Maji Hime | children = Take Tate Ōni no Mikoto (child with Serida Maji Hime), possibly {{ill|Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto|ja|伊迦賀色許売命}} | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} }} '''Utsushikoo no Mikoto''' (内色許男命) is a figure in ancient [[Japan]], identified as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} and referred to as "Utsushikoo no Mikoto" in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and as "Utsushikoo no Mikoto" (欝色雄命) in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}.'' == Overview == In the ''Kojiki'' entry for {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇}}, Utsushikoo no Mikoto is described as the elder brother of the emperor’s consort, {{ill|Utsushikome|lt=Utsushikome no Mikoto|ja|欝色謎命}}, and as the father of {{ill|Ikagashikome|ja|伊迦賀色許売命}}, whom Emperor Kōgen later married. However, traditions associating Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto as the daughter of Utsushikoo no Mikoto are not found in sources other than the ''Kojiki.'' The ''Nihon Shoki'' refers to Utsushikoo as the brother of Utsushikome no Mikoto and founder of the Hozumi clan. Although the ''Kojiki'' and ''Nihon Shoki'' provide limited accounts of his activities, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' states that in the 8th year of Emperor Kōgen’s reign, Utsushikoo no Mikoto was appointed as Grand Minister (Ōomi), while his brother {{ill|Ōhesoki|ja|大綜杵命}} was made Chief Minister (Ōnori). == Notes == {{reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Uchi-irozumi-no-Mikoto}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original {{Infobox Noble | name = Utsushikoo no Mikoto | image = | caption = | period = [[Yayoi period]], [[Ancient Japan]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | alias = Utsushikoo no Mikoto (欝色雄命) | posthumous_name = | deified_name = | burial_place = | rank = [[Ōomi]] (Grand Minister) | sovereign = [[Emperor Kōgen]], [[Emperor Kaika]] | clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}, {{ill|Uneme clan|ja|采女氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja|大矢口宿禰命}}"Tenson Hongi" in {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}.
Mother: Sakadoyuratsuhime | siblings = {{ill|Utsushikome|lt=Utsushikome no Mikoto|ja|欝色謎命}}, Ōmine Ōni no Mikoto | spouse = Serida Maji Hime | children = Take Tate Ōni no Mikoto (child with Serida Maji Hime), possibly {{ill|Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto|ja|伊迦賀色許売命}} | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} }} '''Utsushikoo no Mikoto''' (内色許男命) is a figure in ancient [[Japan]], identified as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} and referred to as "Utsushikoo no Mikoto" in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and as "Utsushikoo no Mikoto" (欝色雄命) in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}.'' == Overview == In the ''Kojiki'' entry for {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇}}, Utsushikoo no Mikoto is described as the elder brother of the emperor’s consort, {{ill|Utsushikome|lt=Utsushikome no Mikoto|ja|欝色謎命}}, and as the father of {{ill|Ikagashikome|ja|伊迦賀色許売命}}, whom Emperor Kōgen later married. However, traditions associating Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto as the daughter of Utsushikoo no Mikoto are not found in sources other than the ''Kojiki.'' The ''Nihon Shoki'' refers to Utsushikoo as the brother of Utsushikome no Mikoto and founder of the Hozumi clan. Although the ''Kojiki'' and ''Nihon Shoki'' provide limited accounts of his activities, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' states that in the 8th year of Emperor Kōgen’s reign, Utsushikoo no Mikoto was appointed as Grand Minister (Ōomi), while his brother {{ill|Ōhesoki|ja|大綜杵命}} was made Chief Minister (Ōnori). == Notes == {{reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} [[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Uchi-irozumi-no-Mikoto}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] {{Infobox Noble | name = Utsushikoo no Mikoto | image = | caption = | period = [[Yayoi period]], [[Ancient Japan]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | alias = Utsushikoo no Mikoto (欝色雄命) | posthumous_name = | deified_name = | burial_place = | rank = [[Ōomi]] (Grand Minister) | sovereign = [[Emperor Kōgen]], [[Emperor Kaika]] | clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}, {{ill|Uneme clan|ja|采女氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja|大矢口宿禰命}}"Tenson Hongi" in {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}.
Mother: Sakadoyuratsuhime | siblings = {{ill|Utsushikome|lt=Utsushikome no Mikoto|ja|欝色謎命}}, Ōmine Ōni no Mikoto | spouse = Serida Maji Hime | children = Take Tate Ōni no Mikoto (child with Serida Maji Hime), possibly {{ill|Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto|ja|伊迦賀色許売命}} | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} }} '''Utsushikoo no Mikoto''' (内色許男命) is a figure in ancient [[Japan]], identified as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} and referred to as "Utsushikoo no Mikoto" in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and as "Utsushikoo no Mikoto" (欝色雄命) in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}.'' == Overview == In the ''Kojiki'' entry for {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇}}, Utsushikoo no Mikoto is described as the elder brother of the emperor’s consort, {{ill|Utsushikome|lt=Utsushikome no Mikoto|ja|欝色謎命}}, and as the father of {{ill|Ikagashikome|ja|伊迦賀色許売命}}, whom Emperor Kōgen later married. However, traditions associating Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto as the daughter of Utsushikoo no Mikoto are not found in sources other than the ''Kojiki.'' The ''Nihon Shoki'' refers to Utsushikoo as the brother of Utsushikome no Mikoto and founder of the Hozumi clan. Although the ''Kojiki'' and ''Nihon Shoki'' provide limited accounts of his activities, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' states that in the 8th year of Emperor Kōgen’s reign, Utsushikoo no Mikoto was appointed as Grand Minister (Ōomi), while his brother {{ill|Ōhesoki|ja|大綜杵命}} was made Chief Minister (Ōnori). == Notes == {{reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Uchi-irozumi-no-Mikoto}}[[category:kk]] {{Infobox Noble | name = Utsushikoo no Mikoto | image = | caption = | period = [[Yayoi period]], [[Ancient Japan]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | alias = Utsushikoo no Mikoto (欝色雄命) | posthumous_name = | deified_name = | burial_place = | rank = [[Ōomi]] (Grand Minister) | sovereign = [[Emperor Kōgen]], [[Emperor Kaika]] | clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}, {{ill|Uneme clan|ja|采女氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja|大矢口宿禰命}}"Tenson Hongi" in {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}.
Mother: Sakadoyuratsuhime | siblings = {{ill|Utsushikome|lt=Utsushikome no Mikoto|ja|欝色謎命}}, Ōmine Ōni no Mikoto | spouse = Serida Maji Hime | children = Take Tate Ōni no Mikoto (child with Serida Maji Hime), possibly {{ill|Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto|ja|伊迦賀色許売命}} | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} }} '''Utsushikoo no Mikoto''' (内色許男命) is a figure in ancient [[Japan]], identified as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} and referred to as "Utsushikoo no Mikoto" in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and as "Utsushikoo no Mikoto" (欝色雄命) in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}.'' == Overview == In the ''Kojiki'' entry for {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇}}, Utsushikoo no Mikoto is described as the elder brother of the emperor’s consort, {{ill|Utsushikome|lt=Utsushikome no Mikoto|ja|欝色謎命}}, and as the father of {{ill|Ikagashikome|ja|伊迦賀色許売命}}, whom Emperor Kōgen later married. However, traditions associating Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto as the daughter of Utsushikoo no Mikoto are not found in sources other than the ''Kojiki.'' The ''Nihon Shoki'' refers to Utsushikoo as the brother of Utsushikome no Mikoto and founder of the Hozumi clan. Although the ''Kojiki'' and ''Nihon Shoki'' provide limited accounts of his activities, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' states that in the 8th year of Emperor Kōgen’s reign, Utsushikoo no Mikoto was appointed as Grand Minister (Ōomi), while his brother {{ill|Ōhesoki|ja|大綜杵命}} was made Chief Minister (Ōnori). == Notes == {{reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] [[Category:fix the O]]{{DEFAULTSORTUchi-irozumi-no-Mikoto}}[[category:fix the O]] {{Infobox Noble | name = Utsushikoo no Mikoto | image = | caption = | period = [[Yayoi period]], [[Ancient Japan]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | alias = Utsushikoo no Mikoto (欝色雄命) | posthumous_name = | deified_name = | burial_place = | rank = [[Ōomi]] (Grand Minister) | sovereign = [[Emperor Kōgen]], [[Emperor Kaika]] | clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}, {{ill|Uneme clan|ja|采女氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja|大矢口宿禰命}}"Tenson Hongi" in {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}.
Mother: Sakadoyuratsuhime | siblings = {{ill|Utsushikome|lt=Utsushikome no Mikoto|ja|欝色謎命}}, Ōmine Ōni no Mikoto | spouse = Serida Maji Hime | children = Take Tate Ōni no Mikoto (child with Serida Maji Hime), possibly {{ill|Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto|ja|伊迦賀色許売命}} | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} }} '''Utsushikoo no Mikoto''' (内色許男命) is a figure in ancient [[Japan]], identified as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} and referred to as "Utsushikoo no Mikoto" in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and as "Utsushikoo no Mikoto" (欝色雄命) in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}.'' == Overview == In the ''Kojiki'' entry for {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇}}, Utsushikoo no Mikoto is described as the elder brother of the emperor’s consort, {{ill|Utsushikome|lt=Utsushikome no Mikoto|ja|欝色謎命}}, and as the father of {{ill|Ikagashikome|ja|伊迦賀色許売命}}, whom Emperor Kōgen later married. However, traditions associating Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto as the daughter of Utsushikoo no Mikoto are not found in sources other than the ''Kojiki.'' The ''Nihon Shoki'' refers to Utsushikoo as the brother of Utsushikome no Mikoto and founder of the Hozumi clan. Although the ''Kojiki'' and ''Nihon Shoki'' provide limited accounts of his activities, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' states that in the 8th year of Emperor Kōgen’s reign, Utsushikoo no Mikoto was appointed as Grand Minister (Ōomi), while his brother {{ill|Ōhesoki|ja|大綜杵命}} was made Chief Minister (Ōnori). == Notes == {{reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} * {{Infobox Noble | name = Utsushikoo no Mikoto | image = | caption = | period = [[Yayoi period]], [[Ancient Japan]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | alias = Utsushikoo no Mikoto (欝色雄命) | posthumous_name = | deified_name = | burial_place = | rank = [[Ōomi]] (Grand Minister) | sovereign = [[Emperor Kōgen]], [[Emperor Kaika]] | clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}, {{ill|Uneme clan|ja|采女氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja|大矢口宿禰命}}"Tenson Hongi" in {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}.
Mother: Sakadoyuratsuhime | siblings = {{ill|Utsushikome|lt=Utsushikome no Mikoto|ja|欝色謎命}}, Ōmine Ōni no Mikoto | spouse = Serida Maji Hime | children = Take Tate Ōni no Mikoto (child with Serida Maji Hime), possibly {{ill|Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto|ja|伊迦賀色許売命}} | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} }} '''Utsushikoo no Mikoto''' (内色許男命) is a figure in ancient [[Japan]], identified as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} and referred to as "Utsushikoo no Mikoto" in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and as "Utsushikoo no Mikoto" (欝色雄命) in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}.'' == Overview == In the ''Kojiki'' entry for {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇}}, Utsushikoo no Mikoto is described as the elder brother of the emperor’s consort, {{ill|Utsushikome|lt=Utsushikome no Mikoto|ja|欝色謎命}}, and as the father of {{ill|Ikagashikome|ja|伊迦賀色許売命}}, whom Emperor Kōgen later married. However, traditions associating Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto as the daughter of Utsushikoo no Mikoto are not found in sources other than the ''Kojiki.'' The ''Nihon Shoki'' refers to Utsushikoo as the brother of Utsushikome no Mikoto and founder of the Hozumi clan. Although the ''Kojiki'' and ''Nihon Shoki'' provide limited accounts of his activities, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' states that in the 8th year of Emperor Kōgen’s reign, Utsushikoo no Mikoto was appointed as Grand Minister (Ōomi), while his brother {{ill|Ōhesoki|ja|大綜杵命}} was made Chief Minister (Ōnori). == Notes == {{reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Uchi-irozumi-no-Mikoto}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{Infobox Noble | name = Utsushikoo no Mikoto | image = | caption = | period = [[Yayoi period]], [[Ancient Japan]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | alias = Utsushikoo no Mikoto (欝色雄命) | posthumous_name = | deified_name = | burial_place = | rank = [[Ōomi]] (Grand Minister) | sovereign = [[Emperor Kōgen]], [[Emperor Kaika]] | clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}, {{ill|Uneme clan|ja|采女氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja|大矢口宿禰命}}"Tenson Hongi" in {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}.
Mother: Sakadoyuratsuhime | siblings = {{ill|Utsushikome|lt=Utsushikome no Mikoto|ja|欝色謎命}}, Ōmine Ōni no Mikoto | spouse = Serida Maji Hime | children = Take Tate Ōni no Mikoto (child with Serida Maji Hime), possibly {{ill|Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto|ja|伊迦賀色許売命}} | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} }} '''Utsushikoo no Mikoto''' (内色許男命) is a figure in ancient [[Japan]], identified as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} and referred to as "Utsushikoo no Mikoto" in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and as "Utsushikoo no Mikoto" (欝色雄命) in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}.'' == Overview == In the ''Kojiki'' entry for {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇}}, Utsushikoo no Mikoto is described as the elder brother of the emperor’s consort, {{ill|Utsushikome|lt=Utsushikome no Mikoto|ja|欝色謎命}}, and as the father of {{ill|Ikagashikome|ja|伊迦賀色許売命}}, whom Emperor Kōgen later married. However, traditions associating Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto as the daughter of Utsushikoo no Mikoto are not found in sources other than the ''Kojiki.'' The ''Nihon Shoki'' refers to Utsushikoo as the brother of Utsushikome no Mikoto and founder of the Hozumi clan. Although the ''Kojiki'' and ''Nihon Shoki'' provide limited accounts of his activities, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' states that in the 8th year of Emperor Kōgen’s reign, Utsushikoo no Mikoto was appointed as Grand Minister (Ōomi), while his brother {{ill|Ōhesoki|ja|大綜杵命}} was made Chief Minister (Ōnori). == Notes == {{reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Uchi-irozumi-no-Mikoto}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{citation needed|Uchi-irozumi-no-Mikoto}} [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Uchi-irozumi-no-Mikoto}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] revision.text:
{{Infobox Noble | name = Utsushikoo no Mikoto | image = | caption = | period = [[Yayoi period]], [[Ancient Japan]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | alias = Utsushikoo no Mikoto (欝色雄命) | posthumous_name = | deified_name = | burial_place = | rank = [[Ōomi]] (Grand Minister) | sovereign = [[Emperor Kōgen]], [[Emperor Kaika]] | clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}, {{ill|Uneme clan|ja|采女氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja|大矢口宿禰命}}"Tenson Hongi" in {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}.
Mother: Sakadoyuratsuhime | siblings = {{ill|Utsushikome|lt=Utsushikome no Mikoto|ja|欝色謎命}}, Ōmine Ōni no Mikoto | spouse = Serida Maji Hime | children = Take Tate Ōni no Mikoto (child with Serida Maji Hime), possibly {{ill|Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto|ja|伊迦賀色許売命}} | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} }} '''Utsushikoo no Mikoto''' (内色許男命) is a figure in ancient [[Japan]], identified as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} and referred to as "Utsushikoo no Mikoto" in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and as "Utsushikoo no Mikoto" (欝色雄命) in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}.'' == Overview == In the ''Kojiki'' entry for {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇}}, Utsushikoo no Mikoto is described as the elder brother of the emperor’s consort, {{ill|Utsushikome|lt=Utsushikome no Mikoto|ja|欝色謎命}}, and as the father of {{ill|Ikagashikome|ja|伊迦賀色許売命}}, whom Emperor Kōgen later married. However, traditions associating Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto as the daughter of Utsushikoo no Mikoto are not found in sources other than the ''Kojiki.'' The ''Nihon Shoki'' refers to Utsushikoo as the brother of Utsushikome no Mikoto and founder of the Hozumi clan. Although the ''Kojiki'' and ''Nihon Shoki'' provide limited accounts of his activities, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' states that in the 8th year of Emperor Kōgen’s reign, Utsushikoo no Mikoto was appointed as Grand Minister (Ōomi), while his brother {{ill|Ōhesoki|ja|大綜杵命}} was made Chief Minister (Ōnori). == Notes == {{reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Uchi-irozumi-no-Mikoto}}[[Category:Unsorted completion {{Infobox Noble | name = Utsushikoo no Mikoto | image = | caption = | period = [[Yayoi period]], [[Ancient Japan]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | alias = Utsushikoo no Mikoto (欝色雄命) | posthumous_name = | deified_name = | burial_place = | rank = [[Ōomi]] (Grand Minister) | sovereign = [[Emperor Kōgen]], [[Emperor Kaika]] | clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}, {{ill|Uneme clan|ja|采女氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja|大矢口宿禰命}}"Tenson Hongi" in {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}.
Mother: Sakadoyuratsuhime | siblings = {{ill|Utsushikome|lt=Utsushikome no Mikoto|ja|欝色謎命}}, Ōmine Ōni no Mikoto | spouse = Serida Maji Hime | children = Take Tate Ōni no Mikoto (child with Serida Maji Hime), possibly {{ill|Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto|ja|伊迦賀色許売命}} | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} }} '''Utsushikoo no Mikoto''' (内色許男命) is a figure in ancient [[Japan]], identified as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} and referred to as "Utsushikoo no Mikoto" in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and as "Utsushikoo no Mikoto" (欝色雄命) in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}.'' == Overview == In the ''Kojiki'' entry for {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇}}, Utsushikoo no Mikoto is described as the elder brother of the emperor’s consort, {{ill|Utsushikome|lt=Utsushikome no Mikoto|ja|欝色謎命}}, and as the father of {{ill|Ikagashikome|ja|伊迦賀色許売命}}, whom Emperor Kōgen later married. However, traditions associating Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto as the daughter of Utsushikoo no Mikoto are not found in sources other than the ''Kojiki.'' The ''Nihon Shoki'' refers to Utsushikoo as the brother of Utsushikome no Mikoto and founder of the Hozumi clan. Although the ''Kojiki'' and ''Nihon Shoki'' provide limited accounts of his activities, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' states that in the 8th year of Emperor Kōgen’s reign, Utsushikoo no Mikoto was appointed as Grand Minister (Ōomi), while his brother {{ill|Ōhesoki|ja|大綜杵命}} was made Chief Minister (Ōnori). == Notes == {{reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Uchi-irozumi-no-Mikoto}}[[Category:Unsorted completion {{Infobox Noble | name = Utsushikoo no Mikoto | image = | caption = | period = [[Yayoi period]], [[Ancient Japan]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | alias = Utsushikoo no Mikoto (欝色雄命) | posthumous_name = | deified_name = | burial_place = | rank = [[Ōomi]] (Grand Minister) | sovereign = [[Emperor Kōgen]], [[Emperor Kaika]] | clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}, {{ill|Uneme clan|ja|采女氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja|大矢口宿禰命}}"Tenson Hongi" in {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}.
Mother: Sakadoyuratsuhime | siblings = {{ill|Utsushikome|lt=Utsushikome no Mikoto|ja|欝色謎命}}, Ōmine Ōni no Mikoto | spouse = Serida Maji Hime | children = Take Tate Ōni no Mikoto (child with Serida Maji Hime), possibly {{ill|Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto|ja|伊迦賀色許売命}} | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} }} '''Utsushikoo no Mikoto''' (内色許男命) is a figure in ancient [[Japan]], identified as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} and referred to as "Utsushikoo no Mikoto" in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and as "Utsushikoo no Mikoto" (欝色雄命) in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}.'' == Overview == In the ''Kojiki'' entry for {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇}}, Utsushikoo no Mikoto is described as the elder brother of the emperor’s consort, {{ill|Utsushikome|lt=Utsushikome no Mikoto|ja|欝色謎命}}, and as the father of {{ill|Ikagashikome|ja|伊迦賀色許売命}}, whom Emperor Kōgen later married. However, traditions associating Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto as the daughter of Utsushikoo no Mikoto are not found in sources other than the ''Kojiki.'' The ''Nihon Shoki'' refers to Utsushikoo as the brother of Utsushikome no Mikoto and founder of the Hozumi clan. Although the ''Kojiki'' and ''Nihon Shoki'' provide limited accounts of his activities, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' states that in the 8th year of Emperor Kōgen’s reign, Utsushikoo no Mikoto was appointed as Grand Minister (Ōomi), while his brother {{ill|Ōhesoki|ja|大綜杵命}} was made Chief Minister (Ōnori). == Notes == {{reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} * {{Infobox Noble | name = Utsushikoo no Mikoto | image = | caption = | period = [[Yayoi period]], [[Ancient Japan]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | alias = Utsushikoo no Mikoto (欝色雄命) | posthumous_name = | deified_name = | burial_place = | rank = [[Ōomi]] (Grand Minister) | sovereign = [[Emperor Kōgen]], [[Emperor Kaika]] | clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}, {{ill|Uneme clan|ja|采女氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja|大矢口宿禰命}}"Tenson Hongi" in {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}.
Mother: Sakadoyuratsuhime | siblings = {{ill|Utsushikome|lt=Utsushikome no Mikoto|ja|欝色謎命}}, Ōmine Ōni no Mikoto | spouse = Serida Maji Hime | children = Take Tate Ōni no Mikoto (child with Serida Maji Hime), possibly {{ill|Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto|ja|伊迦賀色許売命}} | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} }} '''Utsushikoo no Mikoto''' (内色許男命) is a figure in ancient [[Japan]], identified as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} and referred to as "Utsushikoo no Mikoto" in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and as "Utsushikoo no Mikoto" (欝色雄命) in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}.'' == Overview == In the ''Kojiki'' entry for {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇}}, Utsushikoo no Mikoto is described as the elder brother of the emperor’s consort, {{ill|Utsushikome|lt=Utsushikome no Mikoto|ja|欝色謎命}}, and as the father of {{ill|Ikagashikome|ja|伊迦賀色許売命}}, whom Emperor Kōgen later married. However, traditions associating Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto as the daughter of Utsushikoo no Mikoto are not found in sources other than the ''Kojiki.'' The ''Nihon Shoki'' refers to Utsushikoo as the brother of Utsushikome no Mikoto and founder of the Hozumi clan. Although the ''Kojiki'' and ''Nihon Shoki'' provide limited accounts of his activities, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' states that in the 8th year of Emperor Kōgen’s reign, Utsushikoo no Mikoto was appointed as Grand Minister (Ōomi), while his brother {{ill|Ōhesoki|ja|大綜杵命}} was made Chief Minister (Ōnori). == Notes == {{reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} * {{Infobox Noble | name = Utsushikoo no Mikoto | image = | caption = | period = [[Yayoi period]], [[Ancient Japan]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | alias = Utsushikoo no Mikoto (欝色雄命) | posthumous_name = | deified_name = | burial_place = | rank = [[Ōomi]] (Grand Minister) | sovereign = [[Emperor Kōgen]], [[Emperor Kaika]] | clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}, {{ill|Uneme clan|ja|采女氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja|大矢口宿禰命}}"Tenson Hongi" in {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}.
Mother: Sakadoyuratsuhime | siblings = {{ill|Utsushikome|lt=Utsushikome no Mikoto|ja|欝色謎命}}, Ōmine Ōni no Mikoto | spouse = Serida Maji Hime | children = Take Tate Ōni no Mikoto (child with Serida Maji Hime), possibly {{ill|Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto|ja|伊迦賀色許売命}} | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} }} '''Utsushikoo no Mikoto''' (内色許男命) is a figure in ancient [[Japan]], identified as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} and referred to as "Utsushikoo no Mikoto" in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and as "Utsushikoo no Mikoto" (欝色雄命) in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}.'' == Overview == In the ''Kojiki'' entry for {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇}}, Utsushikoo no Mikoto is described as the elder brother of the emperor’s consort, {{ill|Utsushikome|lt=Utsushikome no Mikoto|ja|欝色謎命}}, and as the father of {{ill|Ikagashikome|ja|伊迦賀色許売命}}, whom Emperor Kōgen later married. However, traditions associating Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto as the daughter of Utsushikoo no Mikoto are not found in sources other than the ''Kojiki.'' The ''Nihon Shoki'' refers to Utsushikoo as the brother of Utsushikome no Mikoto and founder of the Hozumi clan. Although the ''Kojiki'' and ''Nihon Shoki'' provide limited accounts of his activities, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' states that in the 8th year of Emperor Kōgen’s reign, Utsushikoo no Mikoto was appointed as Grand Minister (Ōomi), while his brother {{ill|Ōhesoki|ja|大綜杵命}} was made Chief Minister (Ōnori). == Notes == {{reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} * {{Infobox Noble | name = Utsushikoo no Mikoto | image = | caption = | period = [[Yayoi period]], [[Ancient Japan]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | alias = Utsushikoo no Mikoto (欝色雄命) | posthumous_name = | deified_name = | burial_place = | rank = [[Ōomi]] (Grand Minister) | sovereign = [[Emperor Kōgen]], [[Emperor Kaika]] | clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}, {{ill|Uneme clan|ja|采女氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja|大矢口宿禰命}}"Tenson Hongi" in {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}.
Mother: Sakadoyuratsuhime | siblings = {{ill|Utsushikome|lt=Utsushikome no Mikoto|ja|欝色謎命}}, Ōmine Ōni no Mikoto | spouse = Serida Maji Hime | children = Take Tate Ōni no Mikoto (child with Serida Maji Hime), possibly {{ill|Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto|ja|伊迦賀色許売命}} | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} }} '''Utsushikoo no Mikoto''' (内色許男命) is a figure in ancient [[Japan]], identified as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} and referred to as "Utsushikoo no Mikoto" in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and as "Utsushikoo no Mikoto" (欝色雄命) in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}.'' == Overview == In the ''Kojiki'' entry for {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇}}, Utsushikoo no Mikoto is described as the elder brother of the emperor’s consort, {{ill|Utsushikome|lt=Utsushikome no Mikoto|ja|欝色謎命}}, and as the father of {{ill|Ikagashikome|ja|伊迦賀色許売命}}, whom Emperor Kōgen later married. However, traditions associating Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto as the daughter of Utsushikoo no Mikoto are not found in sources other than the ''Kojiki.'' The ''Nihon Shoki'' refers to Utsushikoo as the brother of Utsushikome no Mikoto and founder of the Hozumi clan. Although the ''Kojiki'' and ''Nihon Shoki'' provide limited accounts of his activities, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' states that in the 8th year of Emperor Kōgen’s reign, Utsushikoo no Mikoto was appointed as Grand Minister (Ōomi), while his brother {{ill|Ōhesoki|ja|大綜杵命}} was made Chief Minister (Ōnori). == Notes == {{reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} * The ''Nihon Shoki'' refers to Utsushikoo as the brother of Utsushikome no Mikoto and founder of the Hozumi clan. Although the ''Kojiki'' and ''Nihon Shoki'' provide limited accounts of his activities, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' states that in the 8th year of Emperor Kōgen’s reign, Utsushikoo no Mikoto was appointed as Grand Minister (Ōomi), while his brother {{ill|Ōhesoki|ja|大綜杵命}} was made Chief Minister (Ōnori). == Notes == {{reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} * {{Infobox Noble | name = Utsushikoo no Mikoto | image = | caption = | period = [[Yayoi period]], [[Ancient Japan]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | alias = Utsushikoo no Mikoto (欝色雄命) | posthumous_name = | deified_name = | burial_place = | rank = [[Ōomi]] (Grand Minister) | sovereign = [[Emperor Kōgen]], [[Emperor Kaika]] | clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}, {{ill|Uneme clan|ja|采女氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja|大矢口宿禰命}}"Tenson Hongi" in {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}.
Mother: Sakadoyuratsuhime | siblings = {{ill|Utsushikome|lt=Utsushikome no Mikoto|ja|欝色謎命}}, Ōmine Ōni no Mikoto | spouse = Serida Maji Hime | children = Take Tate Ōni no Mikoto (child with Serida Maji Hime), possibly {{ill|Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto|ja|伊迦賀色許売命}} | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} }} '''Utsushikoo no Mikoto''' (内色許男命) is a figure in ancient [[Japan]], identified as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} and referred to as "Utsushikoo no Mikoto" in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and as "Utsushikoo no Mikoto" (欝色雄命) in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}.'' == Overview == In the ''Kojiki'' entry for {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇}}, Utsushikoo no Mikoto is described as the elder brother of the emperor’s consort, {{ill|Utsushikome|lt=Utsushikome no Mikoto|ja|欝色謎命}}, and as the father of {{ill|Ikagashikome|ja|伊迦賀色許売命}}, whom Emperor Kōgen later married. However, traditions associating Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto as the daughter of Utsushikoo no Mikoto are not found in sources other than the ''Kojiki.'' The ''Nihon Shoki'' refers to Utsushikoo as the brother of Utsushikome no Mikoto and founder of the Hozumi clan. Although the ''Kojiki'' and ''Nihon Shoki'' provide limited accounts of his activities, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' states that in the 8th year of Emperor Kōgen’s reign, Utsushikoo no Mikoto was appointed as Grand Minister (Ōomi), while his brother {{ill|Ōhesoki|ja|大綜杵命}} was made Chief Minister (Ōnori). == Notes == {{reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} * {{Infobox Noble | name = Utsushikoo no Mikoto | image = | caption = | period = [[Yayoi period]], [[Ancient Japan]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | alias = Utsushikoo no Mikoto (欝色雄命) | posthumous_name = | deified_name = | burial_place = | rank = [[Ōomi]] (Grand Minister) | sovereign = [[Emperor Kōgen]], [[Emperor Kaika]] | clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}, {{ill|Uneme clan|ja|采女氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja|大矢口宿禰命}}"Tenson Hongi" in {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}.
Mother: Sakadoyuratsuhime | siblings = {{ill|Utsushikome|lt=Utsushikome no Mikoto|ja|欝色謎命}}, Ōmine Ōni no Mikoto | spouse = Serida Maji Hime | children = Take Tate Ōni no Mikoto (child with Serida Maji Hime), possibly {{ill|Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto|ja|伊迦賀色許売命}} | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} }} '''Utsushikoo no Mikoto''' (内色許男命) is a figure in ancient [[Japan]], identified as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} and referred to as "Utsushikoo no Mikoto" in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and as "Utsushikoo no Mikoto" (欝色雄命) in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}.'' == Overview == In the ''Kojiki'' entry for {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇}}, Utsushikoo no Mikoto is described as the elder brother of the emperor’s consort, {{ill|Utsushikome|lt=Utsushikome no Mikoto|ja|欝色謎命}}, and as the father of {{ill|Ikagashikome|ja|伊迦賀色許売命}}, whom Emperor Kōgen later married. However, traditions associating Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto as the daughter of Utsushikoo no Mikoto are not found in sources other than the ''Kojiki.'' The ''Nihon Shoki'' refers to Utsushikoo as the brother of Utsushikome no Mikoto and founder of the Hozumi clan. Although the ''Kojiki'' and ''Nihon Shoki'' provide limited accounts of his activities, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' states that in the 8th year of Emperor Kōgen’s reign, Utsushikoo no Mikoto was appointed as Grand Minister (Ōomi), while his brother {{ill|Ōhesoki|ja|大綜杵命}} was made Chief Minister (Ōnori). == Notes == {{reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} * {{Infobox Noble | name = Utsushikoo no Mikoto | image = | caption = | period = [[Yayoi period]], [[Ancient Japan]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | alias = Utsushikoo no Mikoto (欝色雄命) | posthumous_name = | deified_name = | burial_place = | rank = [[Ōomi]] (Grand Minister) | sovereign = [[Emperor Kōgen]], [[Emperor Kaika]] | clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}, {{ill|Uneme clan|ja|采女氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja|大矢口宿禰命}}"Tenson Hongi" in {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}.
Mother: Sakadoyuratsuhime | siblings = {{ill|Utsushikome|lt=Utsushikome no Mikoto|ja|欝色謎命}}, Ōmine Ōni no Mikoto | spouse = Serida Maji Hime | children = Take Tate Ōni no Mikoto (child with Serida Maji Hime), possibly {{ill|Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto|ja|伊迦賀色許売命}} | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} }} '''Utsushikoo no Mikoto''' (内色許男命) is a figure in ancient [[Japan]], identified as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} and referred to as "Utsushikoo no Mikoto" in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and as "Utsushikoo no Mikoto" (欝色雄命) in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}.'' == Overview == In the ''Kojiki'' entry for {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇}}, Utsushikoo no Mikoto is described as the elder brother of the emperor’s consort, {{ill|Utsushikome|lt=Utsushikome no Mikoto|ja|欝色謎命}}, and as the father of {{ill|Ikagashikome|ja|伊迦賀色許売命}}, whom Emperor Kōgen later married. However, traditions associating Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto as the daughter of Utsushikoo no Mikoto are not found in sources other than the ''Kojiki.'' The ''Nihon Shoki'' refers to Utsushikoo as the brother of Utsushikome no Mikoto and founder of the Hozumi clan. Although the ''Kojiki'' and ''Nihon Shoki'' provide limited accounts of his activities, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' states that in the 8th year of Emperor Kōgen’s reign, Utsushikoo no Mikoto was appointed as Grand Minister (Ōomi), while his brother {{ill|Ōhesoki|ja|大綜杵命}} was made Chief Minister (Ōnori). == Notes == {{reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} * {{Infobox Noble | name = Utsushikoo no Mikoto | image = | caption = | period = [[Yayoi period]], [[Ancient Japan]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | alias = Utsushikoo no Mikoto (欝色雄命) | posthumous_name = | deified_name = | burial_place = | rank = [[Ōomi]] (Grand Minister) | sovereign = [[Emperor Kōgen]], [[Emperor Kaika]] | clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}, {{ill|Uneme clan|ja|采女氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja|大矢口宿禰命}}"Tenson Hongi" in {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}.
Mother: Sakadoyuratsuhime | siblings = {{ill|Utsushikome|lt=Utsushikome no Mikoto|ja|欝色謎命}}, Ōmine Ōni no Mikoto | spouse = Serida Maji Hime | children = Take Tate Ōni no Mikoto (child with Serida Maji Hime), possibly {{ill|Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto|ja|伊迦賀色許売命}} | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} }} '''Utsushikoo no Mikoto''' (内色許男命) is a figure in ancient [[Japan]], identified as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} and referred to as "Utsushikoo no Mikoto" in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and as "Utsushikoo no Mikoto" (欝色雄命) in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}.'' == Overview == In the ''Kojiki'' entry for {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇}}, Utsushikoo no Mikoto is described as the elder brother of the emperor’s consort, {{ill|Utsushikome|lt=Utsushikome no Mikoto|ja|欝色謎命}}, and as the father of {{ill|Ikagashikome|ja|伊迦賀色許売命}}, whom Emperor Kōgen later married. However, traditions associating Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto as the daughter of Utsushikoo no Mikoto are not found in sources other than the ''Kojiki.'' The ''Nihon Shoki'' refers to Utsushikoo as the brother of Utsushikome no Mikoto and founder of the Hozumi clan. Although the ''Kojiki'' and ''Nihon Shoki'' provide limited accounts of his activities, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' states that in the 8th year of Emperor Kōgen’s reign, Utsushikoo no Mikoto was appointed as Grand Minister (Ōomi), while his brother {{ill|Ōhesoki|ja|大綜杵命}} was made Chief Minister (Ōnori). == Notes == {{reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} * {{Infobox Noble | name = Utsushikoo no Mikoto | image = | caption = | period = [[Yayoi period]], [[Ancient Japan]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | alias = Utsushikoo no Mikoto (欝色雄命) | posthumous_name = | deified_name = | burial_place = | rank = [[Ōomi]] (Grand Minister) | sovereign = [[Emperor Kōgen]], [[Emperor Kaika]] | clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}, {{ill|Uneme clan|ja|采女氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja|大矢口宿禰命}}"Tenson Hongi" in {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}.
Mother: Sakadoyuratsuhime | siblings = {{ill|Utsushikome|lt=Utsushikome no Mikoto|ja|欝色謎命}}, Ōmine Ōni no Mikoto | spouse = Serida Maji Hime | children = Take Tate Ōni no Mikoto (child with Serida Maji Hime), possibly {{ill|Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto|ja|伊迦賀色許売命}} | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} }} '''Utsushikoo no Mikoto''' (内色許男命) is a figure in ancient [[Japan]], identified as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} and referred to as "Utsushikoo no Mikoto" in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and as "Utsushikoo no Mikoto" (欝色雄命) in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}.'' == Overview == In the ''Kojiki'' entry for {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇}}, Utsushikoo no Mikoto is described as the elder brother of the emperor’s consort, {{ill|Utsushikome|lt=Utsushikome no Mikoto|ja|欝色謎命}}, and as the father of {{ill|Ikagashikome|ja|伊迦賀色許売命}}, whom Emperor Kōgen later married. However, traditions associating Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto as the daughter of Utsushikoo no Mikoto are not found in sources other than the ''Kojiki.'' The ''Nihon Shoki'' refers to Utsushikoo as the brother of Utsushikome no Mikoto and founder of the Hozumi clan. Although the ''Kojiki'' and ''Nihon Shoki'' provide limited accounts of his activities, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' states that in the 8th year of Emperor Kōgen’s reign, Utsushikoo no Mikoto was appointed as Grand Minister (Ōomi), while his brother {{ill|Ōhesoki|ja|大綜杵命}} was made Chief Minister (Ōnori). == Notes == {{reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} * {{Infobox Noble | name = Utsushikoo no Mikoto | image = | caption = | period = [[Yayoi period]], [[Ancient Japan]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | alias = Utsushikoo no Mikoto (欝色雄命) | posthumous_name = | deified_name = | burial_place = | rank = [[Ōomi]] (Grand Minister) | sovereign = [[Emperor Kōgen]], [[Emperor Kaika]] | clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}, {{ill|Uneme clan|ja|采女氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja|大矢口宿禰命}}"Tenson Hongi" in {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}.
Mother: Sakadoyuratsuhime | siblings = {{ill|Utsushikome|lt=Utsushikome no Mikoto|ja|欝色謎命}}, Ōmine Ōni no Mikoto | spouse = Serida Maji Hime | children = Take Tate Ōni no Mikoto (child with Serida Maji Hime), possibly {{ill|Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto|ja|伊迦賀色許売命}} | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} }} '''Utsushikoo no Mikoto''' (内色許男命) is a figure in ancient [[Japan]], identified as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} and referred to as "Utsushikoo no Mikoto" in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and as "Utsushikoo no Mikoto" (欝色雄命) in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}.'' == Overview == In the ''Kojiki'' entry for {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇}}, Utsushikoo no Mikoto is described as the elder brother of the emperor’s consort, {{ill|Utsushikome|lt=Utsushikome no Mikoto|ja|欝色謎命}}, and as the father of {{ill|Ikagashikome|ja|伊迦賀色許売命}}, whom Emperor Kōgen later married. However, traditions associating Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto as the daughter of Utsushikoo no Mikoto are not found in sources other than the ''Kojiki.'' The ''Nihon Shoki'' refers to Utsushikoo as the brother of Utsushikome no Mikoto and founder of the Hozumi clan. Although the ''Kojiki'' and ''Nihon Shoki'' provide limited accounts of his activities, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' states that in the 8th year of Emperor Kōgen’s reign, Utsushikoo no Mikoto was appointed as Grand Minister (Ōomi), while his brother {{ill|Ōhesoki|ja|大綜杵命}} was made Chief Minister (Ōnori). == Notes == {{reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} * Although the ''Kojiki'' and ''Nihon Shoki'' provide limited accounts of his activities, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' states that in the 8th year of Emperor Kōgen’s reign, Utsushikoo no Mikoto was appointed as Grand Minister (Ōomi), while his brother {{ill|Ōhesoki|ja|大綜杵命}} was made Chief Minister (Ōnori). == Notes == {{reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} * {{Infobox Noble | name = Utsushikoo no Mikoto | image = | caption = | period = [[Yayoi period]], [[Ancient Japan]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | alias = Utsushikoo no Mikoto (欝色雄命) | posthumous_name = | deified_name = | burial_place = | rank = [[Ōomi]] (Grand Minister) | sovereign = [[Emperor Kōgen]], [[Emperor Kaika]] | clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}, {{ill|Uneme clan|ja|采女氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja|大矢口宿禰命}}"Tenson Hongi" in {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}.
Mother: Sakadoyuratsuhime | siblings = {{ill|Utsushikome|lt=Utsushikome no Mikoto|ja|欝色謎命}}, Ōmine Ōni no Mikoto | spouse = Serida Maji Hime | children = Take Tate Ōni no Mikoto (child with Serida Maji Hime), possibly {{ill|Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto|ja|伊迦賀色許売命}} | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} }} '''Utsushikoo no Mikoto''' (内色許男命) is a figure in ancient [[Japan]], identified as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} and referred to as "Utsushikoo no Mikoto" in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and as "Utsushikoo no Mikoto" (欝色雄命) in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}.'' == Overview == In the ''Kojiki'' entry for {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇}}, Utsushikoo no Mikoto is described as the elder brother of the emperor’s consort, {{ill|Utsushikome|lt=Utsushikome no Mikoto|ja|欝色謎命}}, and as the father of {{ill|Ikagashikome|ja|伊迦賀色許売命}}, whom Emperor Kōgen later married. However, traditions associating Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto as the daughter of Utsushikoo no Mikoto are not found in sources other than the ''Kojiki.'' The ''Nihon Shoki'' refers to Utsushikoo as the brother of Utsushikome no Mikoto and founder of the Hozumi clan. Although the ''Kojiki'' and ''Nihon Shoki'' provide limited accounts of his activities, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' states that in the 8th year of Emperor Kōgen’s reign, Utsushikoo no Mikoto was appointed as Grand Minister (Ōomi), while his brother {{ill|Ōhesoki|ja|大綜杵命}} was made Chief Minister (Ōnori). == Notes == {{reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} * {{Infobox Noble | name = Utsushikoo no Mikoto | image = | caption = | period = [[Yayoi period]], [[Ancient Japan]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | alias = Utsushikoo no Mikoto (欝色雄命) | posthumous_name = | deified_name = | burial_place = | rank = [[Ōomi]] (Grand Minister) | sovereign = [[Emperor Kōgen]], [[Emperor Kaika]] | clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}, {{ill|Uneme clan|ja|采女氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja|大矢口宿禰命}}"Tenson Hongi" in {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}.
Mother: Sakadoyuratsuhime | siblings = {{ill|Utsushikome|lt=Utsushikome no Mikoto|ja|欝色謎命}}, Ōmine Ōni no Mikoto | spouse = Serida Maji Hime | children = Take Tate Ōni no Mikoto (child with Serida Maji Hime), possibly {{ill|Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto|ja|伊迦賀色許売命}} | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} }} '''Utsushikoo no Mikoto''' (内色許男命) is a figure in ancient [[Japan]], identified as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} and referred to as "Utsushikoo no Mikoto" in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and as "Utsushikoo no Mikoto" (欝色雄命) in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}.'' == Overview == In the ''Kojiki'' entry for {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇}}, Utsushikoo no Mikoto is described as the elder brother of the emperor’s consort, {{ill|Utsushikome|lt=Utsushikome no Mikoto|ja|欝色謎命}}, and as the father of {{ill|Ikagashikome|ja|伊迦賀色許売命}}, whom Emperor Kōgen later married. However, traditions associating Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto as the daughter of Utsushikoo no Mikoto are not found in sources other than the ''Kojiki.'' The ''Nihon Shoki'' refers to Utsushikoo as the brother of Utsushikome no Mikoto and founder of the Hozumi clan. Although the ''Kojiki'' and ''Nihon Shoki'' provide limited accounts of his activities, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' states that in the 8th year of Emperor Kōgen’s reign, Utsushikoo no Mikoto was appointed as Grand Minister (Ōomi), while his brother {{ill|Ōhesoki|ja|大綜杵命}} was made Chief Minister (Ōnori). == Notes == {{reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} * Mother: Sakadoyuratsuhime | siblings = {{ill|Utsushikome|lt=Utsushikome no Mikoto|ja|欝色謎命}}, Ōmine Ōni no Mikoto | spouse = Serida Maji Hime | children = Take Tate Ōni no Mikoto (child with Serida Maji Hime), possibly {{ill|Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto|ja|伊迦賀色許売命}} | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} }} '''Utsushikoo no Mikoto''' (内色許男命) is a figure in ancient [[Japan]], identified as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} and referred to as "Utsushikoo no Mikoto" in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and as "Utsushikoo no Mikoto" (欝色雄命) in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}.'' == Overview == In the ''Kojiki'' entry for {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇}}, Utsushikoo no Mikoto is described as the elder brother of the emperor’s consort, {{ill|Utsushikome|lt=Utsushikome no Mikoto|ja|欝色謎命}}, and as the father of {{ill|Ikagashikome|ja|伊迦賀色許売命}}, whom Emperor Kōgen later married. revision.text:
{{Infobox Noble | name = Utsushikoo no Mikoto | image = | caption = | period = [[Yayoi period]], [[Ancient Japan]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | alias = Utsushikoo no Mikoto (欝色雄命) | posthumous_name = | deified_name = | burial_place = | rank = [[Ōomi]] (Grand Minister) | sovereign = [[Emperor Kōgen]], [[Emperor Kaika]] | clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}, {{ill|Uneme clan|ja|采女氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja|大矢口宿禰命}}"Tenson Hongi" in {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}.
Mother: Sakadoyuratsuhime | siblings = {{ill|Utsushikome|lt=Utsushikome no Mikoto|ja|欝色謎命}}, Ōmine Ōni no Mikoto | spouse = Serida Maji Hime | children = Take Tate Ōni no Mikoto (child with Serida Maji Hime), possibly {{ill|Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto|ja|伊迦賀色許売命}} | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} }} '''Utsushikoo no Mikoto''' (内色許男命) is a figure in ancient [[Japan]], identified as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} and referred to as "Utsushikoo no Mikoto" in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and as "Utsushikoo no Mikoto" (欝色雄命) in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}.'' == Overview == In the ''Kojiki'' entry for {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇}}, Utsushikoo no Mikoto is described as the elder brother of the emperor’s consort, {{ill|Utsushikome|lt=Utsushikome no Mikoto|ja|欝色謎命}}, and as the father of {{ill|Ikagashikome|ja|伊迦賀色許売命}}, whom Emperor Kōgen later married. However, traditions associating Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto as the daughter of Utsushikoo no Mikoto are not found in sources other than the ''Kojiki.'' The ''Nihon Shoki'' refers to Utsushikoo as the brother of Utsushikome no Mikoto and founder of the Hozumi clan. Although the ''Kojiki'' and ''Nihon Shoki'' provide limited accounts of his activities, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' states that in the 8th year of Emperor Kōgen’s reign, Utsushikoo no Mikoto was appointed as Grand Minister (Ōomi), while his brother {{ill|Ōhesoki|ja|大綜杵命}} was made Chief Minister (Ōnori). == Notes == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Uchi-irozumi-no-Mikoto}}[[Category:Unsorted completion {{Infobox Noble | name = Utsushikoo no Mikoto | image = | caption = | period = [[Yayoi period]], [[Ancient Japan]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | alias = Utsushikoo no Mikoto (欝色雄命) | posthumous_name = | deified_name = | burial_place = | rank = [[Ōomi]] (Grand Minister) | sovereign = [[Emperor Kōgen]], [[Emperor Kaika]] | clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}, {{ill|Uneme clan|ja|采女氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja|大矢口宿禰命}}"Tenson Hongi" in {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}.
Mother: Sakadoyuratsuhime | siblings = {{ill|Utsushikome|lt=Utsushikome no Mikoto|ja|欝色謎命}}, Ōmine Ōni no Mikoto | spouse = Serida Maji Hime | children = Take Tate Ōni no Mikoto (child with Serida Maji Hime), possibly {{ill|Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto|ja|伊迦賀色許売命}} | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} }} '''Utsushikoo no Mikoto''' (内色許男命) is a figure in ancient [[Japan]], identified as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} and referred to as "Utsushikoo no Mikoto" in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and as "Utsushikoo no Mikoto" (欝色雄命) in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}.'' == Overview == In the ''Kojiki'' entry for {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇}}, Utsushikoo no Mikoto is described as the elder brother of the emperor’s consort, {{ill|Utsushikome|lt=Utsushikome no Mikoto|ja|欝色謎命}}, and as the father of {{ill|Ikagashikome|ja|伊迦賀色許売命}}, whom Emperor Kōgen later married. However, traditions associating Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto as the daughter of Utsushikoo no Mikoto are not found in sources other than the ''Kojiki.'' The ''Nihon Shoki'' refers to Utsushikoo as the brother of Utsushikome no Mikoto and founder of the Hozumi clan. Although the ''Kojiki'' and ''Nihon Shoki'' provide limited accounts of his activities, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' states that in the 8th year of Emperor Kōgen’s reign, Utsushikoo no Mikoto was appointed as Grand Minister (Ōomi), while his brother {{ill|Ōhesoki|ja|大綜杵命}} was made Chief Minister (Ōnori). == Notes == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} * revision.text:
{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 内色許男命 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[弥生時代]]、[[上古]] | 生誕 = 不詳 | 死没 = 不詳 | 改名 = | 別名 = 欝色雄命 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = [[大臣]] | 主君 = [[孝元天皇]]、[[開化天皇]] | 氏族 = [[穂積氏|穂積臣]]、[[采女氏|采女臣]]祖
| 父母 = 父：[[大矢口宿禰命]]<ref name="tenson">「天孫本紀」『[[先代旧事本紀]]』。</ref>、母：坂戸由良都姫<ref name="tenson"/> | 兄弟 = 内色許男命<ref name="tenson"/>、[[内色許売命]]<ref name="tenson"/>、[[大峯大尼命]]<ref name="tenson"/> | 妻 =芹田真稚姫<ref name="tenson"/> | 子 = 武建大尼命（芹田真稚姫との子）<ref name="tenson"/>、一説：[[伊迦賀色許売命]] | 特記事項 = }} '''内色許男命'''（うつしこおのみこと）は、古代[[日本]]の[[豪族]]・'''[[穂積氏|穂積臣]]'''の祖。『[[古事記]]』の表記であり、『[[日本書紀]]』では'''欝色雄命'''と表記する。
== 概要 == 『[[古事記]]』の[[孝元天皇]]記において、孝元天皇の后の[[欝色謎命|内色許売命]]の兄として登場し、続けて天皇が娘の[[伊香色謎命|伊迦賀色許売命]]も妃として娶ったとの記述があるが、伊迦賀色許売命を内色許男命の娘とする伝承は『古事記』以外に見られない。『[[日本書紀]]』では[[開化天皇]]即位前紀に[[欝色謎命]]を穂積臣の祖・欝色雄命の妹と記す。
『記紀』ではそれ以外に目立った活動は伝えられないが、『[[先代旧事本紀]]』の「天皇本紀」では、孝元天皇即位8年春正月に[[物部氏|物部連公]]の祖・[[ウマシマジ|宇摩志麻治命]]の子孫である欝色雄命を[[大臣]]とし、兄弟の[[大綜麻杵命|大綜杵命]]を大禰としたことが記される。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist|refs= <!-- <ref name="keifu3">中田憲信「物部」『諸系譜』第四冊。</ref> <ref name="shinbetsu">[[中田憲信]]『神別系譜』、[[東京国立博物館]]デジタルライブラリー。</ref> --> }}
== 関連項目 == * [[穂積氏]] * [[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:うつしこおのみこと}} [[Category:日本神話の人物]] [[Category:古代日本の人物]] [[Category:穂積氏]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

| 父母 = 父：[[大矢口宿禰命]]<ref name="tenson">「天孫本紀」『[[先代旧事本紀]]』。</ref>、母：坂戸由良都姫<ref name="tenson"/> | 兄弟 = 内色許男命<ref name="tenson"/>、[[内色許売命]]<ref name="tenson"/>、[[大峯大尼命]]<ref name="tenson"/> | 妻 =芹田真稚姫<ref name="tenson"/> | 子 = 武建大尼命（芹田真稚姫との子）<ref name="tenson"/>、一説：[[伊迦賀色許売命]] | 特記事項 = }} '''内色許男命'''（うつしこおのみこと）は、古代[[日本]]の[[豪族]]・'''[[穂積氏|穂積臣]]'''の祖。『[[古事記]]』の表記であり、『[[日本書紀]]』では'''欝色雄命'''と表記する。
== 概要 == 『[[古事記]]』の[[孝元天皇]]記において、孝元天皇の后の[[欝色謎命|内色許売命]]の兄として登場し、続けて天皇が娘の[[伊香色謎命|伊迦賀色許売命]]も妃として娶ったとの記述があるが、伊迦賀色許売命を内色許男命の娘とする伝承は『古事記』以外に見られない。『[[日本書紀]]』では[[開化天皇]]即位前紀に[[欝色謎命]]を穂積臣の祖・欝色雄命の妹と記す。
『記紀』ではそれ以外に目立った活動は伝えられないが、『[[先代旧事本紀]]』の「天皇本紀」では、孝元天皇即位8年春正月に[[物部氏|物部連公]]の祖・[[ウマシマジ|宇摩志麻治命]]の子孫である欝色雄命を[[大臣]]とし、兄弟の[[大綜麻杵命|大綜杵命]]を大禰としたことが記される。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist|refs= <!-- <ref name="keifu3">中田憲信「物部」『諸系譜』第四冊。</ref> <ref name="shinbetsu">[[中田憲信]]『神別系譜』、[[東京国立博物館]]デジタルライブラリー。</ref> --> }}

{{Infobox Noble | name = Utsushikoo no Mikoto | image = | caption = | period = [[Yayoi period]], [[Ancient Japan]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | alias = Utsushikoo no Mikoto (欝色雄命) | posthumous_name = | deified_name = | burial_place = | Rank = [[Ōomi]] (Grand Minister) | sovereign = [[Emperor Kōgen]], [[Emperor Kaika]] | clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Uneme clan|ja|采女氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja|大矢口宿禰命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}"Tenson Hongi" in {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
Mother: Sakadoyuratsuhime | siblings = {{ill|Utsushikome|lt=Utsushikome no Mikoto|ja|欝色謎命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Ōmine Ōni no Mikoto | spouse = Serida Maji Hime | children = Take Tate Ōni no Mikoto (child with Serida Maji Hime), possibly {{ill|Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto|ja|伊迦賀色許売命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} }} '''Utsushikoo no Mikoto''' (内色許男命) is a figure in ancient [[Japan]], identified as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and referred to as "Utsushikoo no Mikoto" in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'' and as "Utsushikoo no Mikoto" (欝色雄命) in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.'' == Overview == In the ''Kojiki'' entry for {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Utsushikoo no Mikoto is described as the elder brother of the emperor’s consort, {{ill|Utsushikome|lt=Utsushikome no Mikoto|ja|欝色謎命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and as the father of {{ill|Ikagashikome|ja|伊迦賀色許売命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, whom Emperor Kōgen later married. However, traditions associating Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto as the daughter of Utsushikoo no Mikoto are not found in sources other than the ''Kojiki.'' The ''Nihon Shoki'' refers to Utsushikoo as the brother of Utsushikome no Mikoto and founder of the Hozumi clan. Although the ''Kojiki'' and ''Nihon Shoki'' provide limited accounts of his activities, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'' states that in the 8th year of Emperor Kōgen’s reign, Utsushikoo no Mikoto was appointed as Grand Minister (Ōomi), while his brother {{ill|Ōhesoki|ja|大綜杵命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was made Chief Minister (Ōnori). == Notes == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{translated page|ja|内色許男命|version=93497022|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Uchi-irozumi-no-Mikoto]] [[Category:Japanese mythology figures]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese figures]] [[Category:Hozumi clan]] [[Category:Date of birth unknown]] [[Category:Date of death unknown]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[en:Uchi-irozumi-no-Mikoto]] [[sn:Uchi-irozumi-no-Mikoto]] [[ja:内色許男命]] [[sn:chi-irozumi-no-Mikoto]]

{{Infobox Noble | name = Utsushikoo no Mikoto | image = | caption = | period = [[Yayoi period]], [[Ancient Japan]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | alias = Utsushikoo no Mikoto (欝色雄命) | posthumous_name = | deified_name = | burial_place = | Rank = [[Ōomi]] (Grand Minister) | sovereign = [[Emperor Kōgen]], [[Emperor Kaika]] | clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Uneme clan|ja|采女氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja|大矢口宿禰命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}"Tenson Hongi" in {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
Mother: Sakadoyuratsuhime | siblings = {{ill|Utsushikome|lt=Utsushikome no Mikoto|ja|欝色謎命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Ōmine Ōni no Mikoto | spouse = Serida Maji Hime | children = Take Tate Ōni no Mikoto (child with Serida Maji Hime), possibly {{ill|Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto|ja|伊迦賀色許売命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} }} '''Utsushikoo no Mikoto''' (内色許男命) is a figure in ancient [[Japan]], identified as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and referred to as "Utsushikoo no Mikoto" in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'' and as "Utsushikoo no Mikoto" (欝色雄命) in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.'' == Overview == In the ''Kojiki'' entry for {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Utsushikoo no Mikoto is described as the elder brother of the emperor’s consort, {{ill|Utsushikome|lt=Utsushikome no Mikoto|ja|欝色謎命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and as the father of {{ill|Ikagashikome|ja|伊迦賀色許売命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, whom Emperor Kōgen later married. However, traditions associating Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto as the daughter of Utsushikoo no Mikoto are not found in sources other than the ''Kojiki.'' The ''Nihon Shoki'' refers to Utsushikoo as the brother of Utsushikome no Mikoto and founder of the Hozumi clan. Although the ''Kojiki'' and ''Nihon Shoki'' provide limited accounts of his activities, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'' states that in the 8th year of Emperor Kōgen’s reign, Utsushikoo no Mikoto was appointed as Grand Minister (Ōomi), while his brother {{ill|Ōhesoki|ja|大綜杵命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was made Chief Minister (Ōnori). == Notes == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{translated page|ja|内色許男命|version=93497022|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Uchi-irozumi-no-Mikoto]] [[Category:Japanese mythology figures]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese figures]] [[Category:Hozumi clan]] [[Category:Date of birth unknown]] [[Category:Date of death unknown]] [[Category:11]] }} [[en:Uchi-irozumi-no-Mikoto]] [[sn:Uchi-irozumi-no-Mikoto]] [[ja:内色許男命]] [[sn:chi-irozumi-no-Mikoto]]

Mother: Sakadoyuratsuhime | siblings = {{ill|Utsushikome|lt=Utsushikome no Mikoto|ja|欝色謎命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsushikome|qq=}}, Ōmine Ōni no Mikoto | spouse = Serida Maji Hime | children = Take Tate Ōni no Mikoto (child with Serida Maji Hime), possibly {{ill|Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto|ja|伊迦賀色許売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊香色謎命}} | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} }} '''Utsushikoo no Mikoto''' (内色許男命) is a figure in ancient [[:en:Japan|Japan]], identified as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} and referred to as "Utsushikoo no Mikoto" in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'' and as "Utsushikoo no Mikoto" (欝色雄命) in the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]].'' == Overview == In the ''Kojiki'' entry for [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]], Utsushikoo no Mikoto is described as the elder brother of the emperor’s consort, {{ill|Utsushikome|lt=Utsushikome no Mikoto|ja|欝色謎命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsushikome|qq=}}, and as the father of {{ill|Ikagashikome|ja|伊迦賀色許売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashikome|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊香色謎命}}, whom Emperor Kōgen later married. However, traditions associating Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto as the daughter of Utsushikoo no Mikoto are not found in sources other than the ''Kojiki.'' The ''Nihon Shoki'' refers to Utsushikoo as the brother of Utsushikome no Mikoto and founder of the Hozumi clan. Although the ''Kojiki'' and ''Nihon Shoki'' provide limited accounts of his activities, the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' states that in the 8th year of Emperor Kōgen’s reign, Utsushikoo no Mikoto was appointed as Grand Minister (Ōomi), while his brother {{ill|Ōhesoki|ja|大綜杵命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhesoki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大綜麻杵命}} was made Chief Minister (Ōnori). == Notes == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of people before the Asuka period|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|内色許男命|version=93497022|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Date of birth unknown]] [[Category:Date of death unknown]] }} [[sn:Uchi-irozumi-no-Mikoto]] [[ja:内色許男命]] [[sn:chi-irozumi-no-Mikoto]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Year of Birth and Death unknown]] [[Category:Hozumi Clan]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q106106814]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Noble | name = Utsushikoo no Mikoto | image = | caption = | period = [[Yayoi period]], [[Ancient Japan]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | alias = Utsushikoo no Mikoto (欝色雄命) | posthumous_name = | deified_name = | burial_place = | Rank = [[Ōomi]] (Grand Minister) | sovereign = [[Emperor Kōgen]], [[Emperor Kaika]] | clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Uneme clan|ja|采女氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme clan|qq=}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja|大矢口宿禰命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}"Tenson Hongi" in [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]].
Mother: Sakadoyuratsuhime | siblings = {{ill|Utsushikome|lt=Utsushikome no Mikoto|ja|欝色謎命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsushikome|qq=}}, Ōmine Ōni no Mikoto | spouse = Serida Maji Hime | children = Take Tate Ōni no Mikoto (child with Serida Maji Hime), possibly {{ill|Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto|ja|伊迦賀色許売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊香色謎命}} | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} }} '''Utsushikoo no Mikoto''' (内色許男命) is a figure in ancient [[:en:Japan|Japan]], identified as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} and referred to as "Utsushikoo no Mikoto" in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'' and as "Utsushikoo no Mikoto" (欝色雄命) in the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]].'' == Overview == In the ''Kojiki'' entry for [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]], Utsushikoo no Mikoto is described as the elder brother of the emperor’s consort, {{ill|Utsushikome|lt=Utsushikome no Mikoto|ja|欝色謎命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsushikome|qq=}}, and as the father of {{ill|Ikagashikome|ja|伊迦賀色許売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashikome|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊香色謎命}}, whom Emperor Kōgen later married. However, traditions associating Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto as the daughter of Utsushikoo no Mikoto are not found in sources other than the ''Kojiki.'' The ''Nihon Shoki'' refers to Utsushikoo as the brother of Utsushikome no Mikoto and founder of the Hozumi clan. Although the ''Kojiki'' and ''Nihon Shoki'' provide limited accounts of his activities, the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' states that in the 8th year of Emperor Kōgen’s reign, Utsushikoo no Mikoto was appointed as Grand Minister (Ōomi), while his brother {{ill|Ōhesoki|ja|大綜杵命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhesoki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大綜麻杵命}} was made Chief Minister (Ōnori). == Notes == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of people before the Asuka period|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|内色許男命|version=93497022|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Date of birth unknown]] [[Category:Date of death unknown]] }} [[sn:Uchi-irozumi-no-Mikoto]] [[ja:内色許男命]] [[sn:chi-irozumi-no-Mikoto]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Year of Birth and Death unknown]] [[Category:Hozumi Clan]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

Mother: Sakadoyuratsuhime | siblings = {{ill|Utsushikome|lt=Utsushikome no Mikoto|ja|欝色謎命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsushikome|qq=}}, Ōmine Ōni no Mikoto | spouse = Serida Maji Hime | children = Take Tate Ōni no Mikoto (child with Serida Maji Hime), possibly {{ill|Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto|ja|伊迦賀色許売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊香色謎命}} | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} }} '''Utsushikoo no Mikoto''' (内色許男命) is a figure in ancient [[:en:Japan|Japan]], identified as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} and referred to as "Utsushikoo no Mikoto" in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'' and as "Utsushikoo no Mikoto" (欝色雄命) in the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]].'' == Overview == In the ''Kojiki'' entry for [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]], Utsushikoo no Mikoto is described as the elder brother of the emperor’s consort, {{ill|Utsushikome|lt=Utsushikome no Mikoto|ja|欝色謎命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsushikome|qq=}}, and as the father of {{ill|Ikagashikome|ja|伊迦賀色許売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashikome|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊香色謎命}}, whom Emperor Kōgen later married. However, traditions associating Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto as the daughter of Utsushikoo no Mikoto are not found in sources other than the ''Kojiki.'' The ''Nihon Shoki'' refers to Utsushikoo as the brother of Utsushikome no Mikoto and founder of the Hozumi clan. Although the ''Kojiki'' and ''Nihon Shoki'' provide limited accounts of his activities, the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' states that in the 8th year of Emperor Kōgen’s reign, Utsushikoo no Mikoto was appointed as Grand Minister (Ōomi), while his brother {{ill|Ōhesoki|ja|大綜杵命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhesoki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大綜麻杵命}} was made Chief Minister (Ōnori). == Notes == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of people before the Asuka period|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|内色許男命|version=93497022|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Date of birth unknown]] [[Category:Date of death unknown]] }} [[sn:Uchi-irozumi-no-Mikoto]] [[ja:内色許男命]] [[sn:chi-irozumi-no-Mikoto]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]][[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]] [[Category:Hozumi Clan]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Uchi-irozumi-no-Mikoto]][[Category:qq]]

The ''Nihon Shoki'' refers to Utsushikoo as the brother of Utsushikome no Mikoto and founder of the Hozumi clan. Although the ''Kojiki'' and ''Nihon Shoki'' provide limited accounts of his activities, the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' states that in the 8th year of Emperor Kōgen’s reign, Utsushikoo no Mikoto was appointed as Grand Minister (Ōomi), while his brother {{ill|Ōhesoki|ja|大綜杵命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhesoki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大綜麻杵命}} was made Chief Minister (Ōnori). == Notes == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of people before the Asuka period|qq=|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}} {{translated page|ja|内色許男命|version=93497022|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Date of birth unknown]] [[Category:Date of death unknown]] }} [[sn:Uchi-irozumi-no-Mikoto]] [[ja:内色許男命]] [[sn:chi-irozumi-no-Mikoto]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]][[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]] [[Category:Hozumi Clan]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[en:Uchi-irozumi-no-Mikoto]]
