Mother: Sakadoyuratsuhime | siblings = {{ill|Utsushikome|lt=Utsushikome no Mikoto|ja|欝色謎命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsushikome|qq=}}, Ōmine Ōni no Mikoto | spouse = Serida Maji Hime | children = Take Tate Ōni no Mikoto (child with Serida Maji Hime), possibly {{ill|Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto|ja|伊迦賀色許売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊香色謎命|1=Ikagashikome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10885176}} | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} }} '''Utsushikoo no Mikoto''' (内色許男命) is a figure in ancient [[:en:Japan|Japan]], identified as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} and referred to as "Utsushikoo no Mikoto" in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'' and as "Utsushikoo no Mikoto" (欝色雄命) in the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]].'' == Overview == In the ''Kojiki'' entry for [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]], Utsushikoo no Mikoto is described as the elder brother of the emperor’s consort, {{ill|Utsushikome|lt=Utsushikome no Mikoto|ja|欝色謎命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsushikome|qq=}}, and as the father of {{ill|Ikagashikome|ja|伊迦賀色許売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashikome|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊香色謎命}}, whom Emperor Kōgen later married. However, traditions associating Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto as the daughter of Utsushikoo no Mikoto are not found in sources other than the ''Kojiki.'' The ''Nihon Shoki'' refers to Utsushikoo as the brother of Utsushikome no Mikoto and founder of the Hozumi clan. Although the ''Kojiki'' and ''Nihon Shoki'' provide limited accounts of his activities, the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' states that in the 8th year of Emperor Kōgen’s reign, Utsushikoo no Mikoto was appointed as Grand Minister (Ōomi), while his brother {{ill|Ōhesoki|ja|大綜杵命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhesoki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大綜麻杵命}} was made Chief Minister (Ōnori). == Notes == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of people before the Asuka period|qq=|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}} {{translated page|ja|内色許男命|version=93497022|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Date of birth unknown]] [[Category:Date of death unknown]] }} [[ja:内色許男命]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]][[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]] [[Category:Hozumi Clan]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] {{wikidata link|Q106106814}}

Although the ''Kojiki'' and ''Nihon Shoki'' provide limited accounts of his activities, the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' states that in the 8th year of Emperor Kōgen’s reign, Utsushikoo no Mikoto was appointed as Grand Minister (Ōomi), while his brother {{ill|Ōhesoki|ja|大綜杵命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhesoki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大綜麻杵命}} was made Chief Minister (Ōnori). == Notes == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of people before the Asuka period|qq=|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}} {{translated page|ja|内色許男命|version=93497022|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Date of birth unknown]] [[Category:Date of death unknown]] }} [[ja:内色許男命]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]][[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]] [[Category:Hozumi Clan]] {{wikidata link|Q106106814}}

Although the ''Kojiki'' and ''Nihon Shoki'' provide limited accounts of his activities, the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' states that in the 8th year of Emperor Kōgen’s reign, Utsushikoo no Mikoto was appointed as Grand Minister (Ōomi), while his brother {{ill|Ōhesoki|ja|大綜杵命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhesoki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大綜麻杵命}} was made Chief Minister (Ōnori). == Notes == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of people before the Asuka period|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}} {{translated page|ja|内色許男命|version=93497022|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Date of birth unknown]] [[Category:Date of death unknown]] }} [[ja:内色許男命]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]][[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]] [[Category:Hozumi Clan]] {{wikidata link|Q106106814}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

Mother: Sakadoyuratsuhime | siblings = {{ill|Utsushikome|lt=Utsushikome no Mikoto|ja|欝色謎命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsushikome|qq=}}, Ōmine Ōni no Mikoto | spouse = Serida Maji Hime | children = Take Tate Ōni no Mikoto (child with Serida Maji Hime), possibly {{ill|Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto|ja|伊迦賀色許売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊香色謎命|1=Ikagashikome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10885176}} | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} }} '''Utsushikoo no Mikoto''' (内色許男命) is a figure in ancient [[:en:Japan|Japan]], identified as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} and referred to as "Utsushikoo no Mikoto" in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'' and as "Utsushikoo no Mikoto" (欝色雄命) in the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]].'' == Overview == In the ''Kojiki'' entry for [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]], Utsushikoo no Mikoto is described as the elder brother of the emperor’s consort, {{ill|Utsushikome|lt=Utsushikome no Mikoto|ja|欝色謎命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsushikome|qq=}}, and as the father of {{ill|Ikagashikome|ja|伊迦賀色許売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashikome|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊香色謎命}}, whom Emperor Kōgen later married. However, traditions associating Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto as the daughter of Utsushikoo no Mikoto are not found in sources other than the ''Kojiki.'' The ''Nihon Shoki'' refers to Utsushikoo as the brother of Utsushikome no Mikoto and founder of the Hozumi clan. Although the ''Kojiki'' and ''Nihon Shoki'' provide limited accounts of his activities, the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' states that in the 8th year of Emperor Kōgen’s reign, Utsushikoo no Mikoto was appointed as Grand Minister (Ōomi), while his brother {{ill|Ōhesoki|ja|大綜杵命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhesoki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大綜麻杵命}} was made Chief Minister (Ōnori). == Notes == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of people before the Asuka period|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}} {{translated page|ja|内色許男命|version=93497022|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Date of birth unknown]] [[Category:Date of death unknown]] }} [[ja:内色許男命]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]][[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]] [[Category:Hozumi Clan]] {{wikidata link|Q106106814}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

Although the ''Kojiki'' and ''Nihon Shoki'' provide limited accounts of his activities, the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' states that in the 8th year of Emperor Kōgen’s reign, Utsushikoo no Mikoto was appointed as Grand Minister (Ōomi), while his brother {{ill|Ōhesoki|ja|大綜杵命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhesoki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大綜麻杵命}} was made Chief Minister (Ōnori). == Notes == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of people before the Asuka period|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}} {{translated page|ja|内色許男命|version=93497022|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Date of birth unknown]] [[Category:Date of death unknown]] }} [[ja:内色許男命]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]][[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]] [[Category:Hozumi Clan]] {{wikidata link|Q106106814}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[ja:内色許男命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|children={{ill|Q11436785|WD=Q11436785}}|father={{ill|Q106106001|WD=Q106106001}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q106106814]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | name = Utsushikoo no Mikoto | image = | caption = | period = [[Yayoi period]], [[Ancient Japan]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | alias = Utsushikoo no Mikoto (欝色雄命) | posthumous_name = | deified_name = | burial_place = | Rank = [[Ōomi]] (Grand Minister) | sovereign = [[Emperor Kōgen]], [[Emperor Kaika]] | clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Uneme clan|ja|采女氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme clan|1=Uneme Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20038916}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja|大矢口宿禰命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}"Tenson Hongi" in [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]].
Mother: Sakadoyuratsuhime | siblings = {{ill|Utsushikome|lt=Utsushikome no Mikoto|ja|欝色謎命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsushikome|qq=}}, Ōmine Ōni no Mikoto | spouse = Serida Maji Hime | children = Take Tate Ōni no Mikoto (child with Serida Maji Hime), possibly {{ill|Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto|ja|伊迦賀色許売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊香色謎命|1=Ikagashikome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10885176}} | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} }} '''Utsushikoo no Mikoto''' (内色許男命) is a figure in ancient [[:en:Japan|Japan]], identified as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} and referred to as "Utsushikoo no Mikoto" in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'' and as "Utsushikoo no Mikoto" (欝色雄命) in the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]].'' == Overview == In the ''Kojiki'' entry for [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]], Utsushikoo no Mikoto is described as the elder brother of the emperor’s consort, {{ill|Utsushikome|lt=Utsushikome no Mikoto|ja|欝色謎命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsushikome|qq=}}, and as the father of {{ill|Ikagashikome|ja|伊迦賀色許売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashikome|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊香色謎命}}, whom Emperor Kōgen later married. However, traditions associating Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto as the daughter of Utsushikoo no Mikoto are not found in sources other than the ''Kojiki.'' The ''Nihon Shoki'' refers to Utsushikoo as the brother of Utsushikome no Mikoto and founder of the Hozumi clan. Although the ''Kojiki'' and ''Nihon Shoki'' provide limited accounts of his activities, the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' states that in the 8th year of Emperor Kōgen’s reign, Utsushikoo no Mikoto was appointed as Grand Minister (Ōomi), while his brother {{ill|Ōhesoki|ja|大綜杵命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhesoki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大綜麻杵命}} was made Chief Minister (Ōnori). == Notes == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of people before the Asuka period|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}} {{translated page|ja|内色許男命|version=93497022|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Date of birth unknown]] [[Category:Date of death unknown]] }} [[ja:内色許男命]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]][[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]] [[Category:Hozumi Clan]] {{wikidata link|Q106106814}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] {{wikidata link|Q106106814}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Mother: Sakadoyuratsuhime | siblings = {{ill|Utsushikome|lt=Utsushikome no Mikoto|ja|欝色謎命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsushikome|qq=}}, Ōmine Ōni no Mikoto | spouse = Serida Maji Hime | children = Take Tate Ōni no Mikoto (child with Serida Maji Hime), possibly {{ill|Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto|ja|伊迦賀色許売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊香色謎命|1=Ikagashikome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10885176}} | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} }} '''Utsushikoo no Mikoto''' (内色許男命) is a figure in ancient [[:en:Japan|Japan]], identified as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} and referred to as "Utsushikoo no Mikoto" in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'' and as "Utsushikoo no Mikoto" (欝色雄命) in the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]].'' == Overview == In the ''Kojiki'' entry for [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]], Utsushikoo no Mikoto is described as the elder brother of the emperor’s consort, {{ill|Utsushikome|lt=Utsushikome no Mikoto|ja|欝色謎命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsushikome|qq=}}, and as the father of {{ill|Ikagashikome|ja|伊迦賀色許売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashikome|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊香色謎命}}, whom Emperor Kōgen later married. However, traditions associating Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto as the daughter of Utsushikoo no Mikoto are not found in sources other than the ''Kojiki.'' The ''Nihon Shoki'' refers to Utsushikoo as the brother of Utsushikome no Mikoto and founder of the Hozumi clan. Although the ''Kojiki'' and ''Nihon Shoki'' provide limited accounts of his activities, the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' states that in the 8th year of Emperor Kōgen’s reign, Utsushikoo no Mikoto was appointed as Grand Minister (Ōomi), while his brother {{ill|Ōhesoki|ja|大綜杵命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhesoki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大綜麻杵命}} was made Chief Minister (Ōnori). == Notes == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of people before the Asuka period|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}} {{translated page|ja|内色許男命|version=93497022|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Date of birth unknown]] [[Category:Date of death unknown]] }} [[ja:内色許男命]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]][[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]] [[Category:Hozumi Clan]] {{wikidata link|Q106106814}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q106106814}}

Although the ''Kojiki'' and ''Nihon Shoki'' provide limited accounts of his activities, the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' states that in the 8th year of Emperor Kōgen’s reign, Utsushikoo no Mikoto was appointed as Grand Minister (Ōomi), while his brother {{ill|Ōhesoki|ja|大綜杵命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhesoki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大綜麻杵命}} was made Chief Minister (Ōnori). == Notes == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of people before the Asuka period|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}} {{translated page|ja|内色許男命|version=93497022|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Date of birth unknown]] [[Category:Date of death unknown]] }} [[ja:内色許男命]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]][[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]] [[Category:Hozumi Clan]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q106106814}} [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

However, traditions associating Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto as the daughter of Utsushikoo no Mikoto are not found in sources other than the ''Kojiki.'' The ''Nihon Shoki'' refers to Utsushikoo as the brother of Utsushikome no Mikoto and founder of the Hozumi clan. Although the ''Kojiki'' and ''Nihon Shoki'' provide limited accounts of his activities, the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' states that in the 8th year of Emperor Kōgen’s reign, Utsushikoo no Mikoto was appointed as Grand Minister (Ōomi), while his brother {{ill|Ōhesoki|ja|大綜杵命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhesoki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大綜麻杵命}} was made Chief Minister (Ōnori). == Notes == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of people before the Asuka period|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}} {{translated page|ja|内色許男命|version=93497022|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:内色許男命]] {{wikidata link|Q106106814}} [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Date of birth unknown]] [[Category:Date of death unknown]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:Hozumi Clan]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]]

Although the ''Kojiki'' and ''Nihon Shoki'' provide limited accounts of his activities, the ''{{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi}}'' states that in the 8th year of Emperor Kōgen’s reign, Utsushikoo no Mikoto was appointed as Grand Minister (Ōomi), while his brother {{ill|Ōhesoki|ja|大綜杵命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhesoki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大綜麻杵命}} was made Chief Minister (Ōnori). == Notes == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of people before the Asuka period|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}} {{translated page|ja|内色許男命|version=93497022|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:内色許男命]] {{wikidata link|Q106106814}} [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Date of birth unknown]] [[Category:Date of death unknown]] [[Category:Hozumi Clan]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]]

[[ja:内色許男命]] [[ja:内色許男命]] [[ja:内色許男命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|children={{ill|Q11436785|WD=Q11436785}}|father={{ill|Q106106001|WD=Q106106001}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q106106814]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | name = Utsushikoo no Mikoto | image = | caption = | period = [[Yayoi period]], [[Ancient Japan]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | alias = Utsushikoo no Mikoto (欝色雄命) | posthumous_name = | deified_name = | burial_place = | Rank = [[Ōomi]] (Grand Minister) | sovereign = [[Emperor Kōgen]], [[Emperor Kaika]] | clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Uneme clan|ja|采女氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme clan|1=Uneme Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20038916}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja|大矢口宿禰命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}"Tenson Hongi" in {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi}}.
Mother: Sakadoyuratsuhime | siblings = {{ill|Utsushikome|lt=Utsushikome no Mikoto|ja|欝色謎命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsushikome|qq=}}, Ōmine Ōni no Mikoto | spouse = Serida Maji Hime | children = Take Tate Ōni no Mikoto (child with Serida Maji Hime), possibly {{ill|Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto|ja|伊迦賀色許売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊香色謎命|1=Ikagashikome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10885176}} | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} }} '''Utsushikoo no Mikoto''' (内色許男命) is a figure in ancient {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}, identified as the progenitor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} and referred to as "Utsushikoo no Mikoto" in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}'' and as "Utsushikoo no Mikoto" (欝色雄命) in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}.'' == Overview == In the ''Kojiki'' entry for {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|en|Emperor Kōgen|WD=Q333572|lt=Emperor Kōgen}}, Utsushikoo no Mikoto is described as the elder brother of the emperor’s consort, {{ill|Utsushikome|lt=Utsushikome no Mikoto|ja|欝色謎命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsushikome|qq=}}, and as the father of {{ill|Ikagashikome|ja|伊迦賀色許売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashikome|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊香色謎命}}, whom Emperor Kōgen later married. However, traditions associating Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto as the daughter of Utsushikoo no Mikoto are not found in sources other than the ''Kojiki.'' The ''Nihon Shoki'' refers to Utsushikoo as the brother of Utsushikome no Mikoto and founder of the Hozumi clan. Although the ''Kojiki'' and ''Nihon Shoki'' provide limited accounts of his activities, the ''{{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi}}'' states that in the 8th year of Emperor Kōgen’s reign, Utsushikoo no Mikoto was appointed as Grand Minister (Ōomi), while his brother {{ill|Ōhesoki|ja|大綜杵命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhesoki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大綜麻杵命}} was made Chief Minister (Ōnori). == Notes == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of people before the Asuka period|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}} {{translated page|ja|内色許男命|version=93497022|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:内色許男命]] {{wikidata link|Q106106814}} [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Date of birth unknown]] [[Category:Date of death unknown]] [[Category:Hozumi Clan]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]]

However, traditions associating Ikaga Shikome no Mikoto as the daughter of Utsushikoo no Mikoto are not found in sources other than the ''Kojiki.'' The ''Nihon Shoki'' refers to Utsushikoo as the brother of Utsushikome no Mikoto and founder of the Hozumi clan. Although the ''Kojiki'' and ''Nihon Shoki'' provide limited accounts of his activities, the ''{{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi}}'' states that in the 8th year of Emperor Kōgen’s reign, Utsushikoo no Mikoto was appointed as Grand Minister (Ōomi), while his brother {{ill|Ōhesoki|ja|大綜杵命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhesoki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大綜麻杵命}} was made Chief Minister (Ōnori). == Notes == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of people before the Asuka period|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}} {{translated page|ja|内色許男命|version=93497022|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:内色許男命]] {{wikidata link|Q106106814}} [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Date of birth unknown]] [[Category:Date of death unknown]] [[Category:Hozumi Clan]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]]

Although the ''Kojiki'' and ''Nihon Shoki'' provide limited accounts of his activities, the ''{{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}}'' states that in the 8th year of Emperor Kōgen’s reign, Utsushikoo no Mikoto was appointed as Grand Minister (Ōomi), while his brother {{ill|Ōhesoki|ja|大綜杵命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhesoki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大綜麻杵命}} was made Chief Minister (Ōnori). == Notes == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of people before the Asuka period|1=list of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}} {{translated page|ja|内色許男命|version=93497022|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:内色許男命]] {{wikidata link|Q106106814}} [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Date of birth unknown]] [[Category:Date of death unknown]] [[Category:Hozumi Clan]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]] [[Category:pages]]

The ''Nihon Shoki'' refers to Utsushikoo as the brother of Utsushikome no Mikoto and founder of the Hozumi clan.
